name: OpenOps
what_it_does: OpenOps is a no-code FinOps automation platform that helps organizations reduce cloud spend through pre-built and customizable workflows for cost allocation, anomaly detection, workload optimization, and budget management with human-in-the-loop approvals.
use_cases: Automating cloud cost anomaly detection and alerting workflows across AWS, Azure, and GCP
Building approval workflows for rightsizing or reservation recommendations
Centralizing FinOps reporting with spreadsheet-like tables and visualization dashboards
Enforcing tagging policies and auto-remediating untagged resources
not_for: Organizations without meaningful cloud spend (startup/free tier scale)
Teams needing deep programmatic API access over a no-code interface
best_when: A FinOps or cloud engineering team wants to automate cost governance workflows without writing custom code, with built-in human approval gates and audit trails.
avoid_when: The organization needs custom complex logic best expressed in code, or has already invested in a mature Terraform/CSPM toolchain.

auth.notes: Authentication details not specified in README. Cloud provider credentials required for integrations.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 self-hosted option is free. Managed cloud tier pricing not detailed in README.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No MCP server; error handling details not documented.
known_agent_gotchas: AI features referenced but not detailed in README
Cloud provider credential management adds setup complexity
